body.u: My career started when I joined the army I decided that this was what I wanted to do when I was fifteen so I went to the careers office to find out more about the tests I would have to take they told me to apply after Christmas, when I told my parents this, my mother said to me that seems very late, so she phoned up the army careers office at Chatham and they told her that there were only two places left for testing of the next day, so they invited me to go over and take the entrenceentrance test. After I passed the test I was the invited to attend a selection center to find out which section of the army I was best at, my first choice was to go into the Royal Corps of Transport as a senior Driver.

The one thing that let me down was my English result from my exams mind you that not supprisingsurprising when you teachers like Mr Haydon who can’t spell. But even because I had a bad result it didn’t stop me getinggetting in.
This thing that used to bug me was our , sergentsergeant he really was a sqarescare basher just like Mr Hutley.
I always remember what we used to say watch out! Watch out! Hutley’s about.
